watch_out: accommodation WiFi can be unstable even when speed tests look good
islands can be more expensive and less reliable for calls
quiet rooms are not guaranteed in hostels or thin-walled condos
field_notes: Good mobile data backup is essential.
For online teaching or calls, prefer private rooms, cowork booths, or verified quiet apartments.
Bangkok and Chiang Mai are easier than smaller islands for backup work options.

remote_work_zones.name: Bangkok
remote_work_zones.summary: Best all-round option for serious remote work — backup internet, coworking, and late-night convenience.
watch_out: Traffic can make daily movement exhausting.
Condo sound insulation varies a lot.
Cafés can be noisy or unsuitable for calls.

remote_work_zones.name: Chiang Mai
remote_work_zones.summary: Strong budget nomad base with many cafés and a slower rhythm, but seasonality matters.
watch_out: Burning season (Feb–Apr) can seriously affect comfort.
Some older apartments have weak sound insulation.
Internet is usually good but always verify accommodation reviews.

remote_work_zones.name: Koh Phangan
remote_work_zones.summary: Great for flexible remote workers and slow island lifestyle, less ideal for strict daily video calls.
watch_out: Island internet can be inconsistent — always test on arrival.
Backup cowork / café options are more limited than Bangkok or Chiang Mai.
Rainy season and power outages can affect reliability.
